executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations compromised in a single day, April 16, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and National Money Mart (database leaked). The attacks span all critical sectors—healthcare, education, financial services, government, and infrastructure—indicating coordinated or opportunistic mass exploitation. Qilin ransomware leads with 20 victims, followed by CLOP (14), Play (10), and SafePay (10), suggesting either a widespread vulnerability being exploited or compromised credentials/supply chain access.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faced a catastrophic ransomware event on April 16, 2026, with 103 organizations across all provinces compromised. Critical infrastructure targets include two hospitals (Markham Stouffville, McKee-Pownall Equine Services), public health (Peterborough, Thunder Bay Counselling), municipal government (North Perth), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, College Superieur De Montreal), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, Morison Insurance). No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by both Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters; Active Green + Ross, Kids & Company also breached
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest ransomware); Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi

report_2w.executive_summary: Fortinet FortiClientEMS is under active exploitation via CVE-2026-35616, prompting an emergency CCCS advisory and vendor patch. The Axios JavaScript library vulnerability (CVE-2026-40175) enables remote code execution and was exploited to compromise OpenAI's macOS app supply chain, forcing certificate revocation. ShinyHunters breached Rockstar Games via compromised Slack credentials, threatening to leak game development data, while also breaching Hallmark (1.7M accounts) and claiming responsibility for additional attacks. Iranian APT group CyberAv3ngers continues targeting U.S. water utilities and industrial SCADA systems. The FBI dismantled the W3LL phishing operation responsible for $20M in losses.

items.description: Vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS enabling unauthorized access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: CRLF injection vulnerability in Axios JavaScript library enabling remote code execution and credential theft
items.software_affected: Axios JavaScript library (used in OpenAI macOS app and other applications)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games (via Slack compromise), Hallmark (1.7M accounts via Salesforce), multiple other victims
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship - third-party compromise), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service - Slack), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - ransomware/extortion)

items.name: CyberAv3ngers (Iranian APT)
items.targets: U.S. water utilities, Unitronics industrial SCADA controllers
items.ttps: T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1491 (Defacement), targeting critical infrastructure ICS/SCADA systems

items.name: W3LL Phishing Operation
items.targets: Multiple organizations via phishing campaigns totaling $20M in losses
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), credential harvesting and business email compromise

items.name: CozyDuke (APT29 / MiniDionis campaign)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), utilizing CozyCar/SeaDuke malware variants

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion ransomware

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses), double extortion tactics

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), data exfiltration and encryption

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued emergency advisory AL26-007 regarding active exploitation of Fortinet FortiClientEMS CVE-2026-35616, urging immediate patching. Operation Atlantic, involving Canadian law enforcement, seized $12M in cryptocurrency linked to phishing operations. No direct breaches of major Canadian retail or financial institutions were reported in this period.
